title: Timothée Chalamet Loses Best Actor at 2026 Oscars
text: At the 98th Academy Awards on March 15, 2026, Timothée Chalamet did not win the Best Actor award despite being considered a frontrunner throughout awards season. His role in A24's Marty Supreme, where he portrayed ping-pong hustler Marty Mauser, earned him his third Oscar nomination in this category. The award went to Michael B. Jordan for Sinners. Chalamet responded graciously, as cameras captured him all smiles, clapping to support Jordan's win. Awards Season Performance: Chalamet had been dominant leading up to the Oscars, having won both the Golden Globe and Critics Choice awards for the same role. However, Marty Supreme ultimately left the ceremony empty-handed despite receiving nine total nominations. Notable Moments: Conan O'Brien, the ceremony's host, joked about Chalamet's recent controversial comments regarding ballet and opera. O'Brien quipped: Security is extremely tight tonight. I'm told there are concerned about attacks from both the opera and ballet community. He added that they were just mad you left out jazz, drawing laughs from Chalamet and his plus-one, Kylie Jenner. The actress attended for the second consecutive year as Chalamet's guest, wearing a fiery red dress rather than the film's signature orange branding.
